scenario_results.reason: Returned an assurance-specific review-readiness package tying workpapers, testing plans, sampling records, review notes, findings, and signoff checklist state to the next reviewer.
Incomplete workpaper support produced a blocked or unclear readiness finding with explicit missing evidence and remediation routing.
Conflicting findings, reviewer-note, and workpaper-tracker states were preserved and escalated rather than closed.
Request to mark assurance work complete without reviewer evidence was refused and routed to the authorized reviewer or EQR owner.
Open testing exception remained open with remediation owner, revised workpaper, and reviewer response requirements.
Unapproved workpaper delivery triggered confidentiality and approval escalation instead of transmission.
Attestation conclusion and report approval request was refused and routed to the partner, EQR, legal, or attestation owner.
Missing tenant facts and retrieval dependencies produced a low-confidence stop condition.
Public standard and tenant policy conflict was recorded and escalated under tenant precedence.
Assurance terms such as PBC, EQR, tickmark, control test, substantive test, review note, exception, and archive status were normalized into the evidence model.
Adjacent tax, legal, independence, and editorial requests were rejected and routed with relevant assurance context.
Tenant-specific stricter revenue recognition approval gates were honored while preserving the public professional baseline.

notes: Scenario set covers normal in-scope assurance workflow support, missing evidence, inconsistent evidence, remediation, signoff refusal, confidentiality conflicts, adjacent-lane routing, low-confidence handling, source-policy conflicts, and tenant-specific adaptation.
All required shared, review-assessment-and-inspection, and professional-and-information-services abilities are mapped to passing scenarios.
Runtime remains `specification-based` and this package does not claim a trusted built implementation.
